section_name: Protection of Financial and State Rights of Tamil Nadu [4]
promise: Implementation of a Caste Survey to ensure proportional representation [4].

section_name: Protection of Financial and State Rights of Tamil Nadu [4]
promise: Demanding a fair financial devolution from the Union Government, fighting for Tamil Nadu's rightful share up to the Supreme Court [4].

section_name: Protection of Financial and State Rights of Tamil Nadu [4]
promise: Securing a fair share of GST revenue by advocating for 70% to be allocated to states [4].

section_name: Protection of Financial and State Rights of Tamil Nadu [4]
promise: Taking legal and parliamentary steps to transfer Education back to the State List [4].

section_name: Protection of Financial and State Rights of Tamil Nadu [4]
promise: Strongly opposing constituency delimitation based on population to protect Southern states' representation [5].

promise: Opposing the 'One Nation, One Election' proposal which weakens state autonomy [5].

promise: Protecting the Two-Language Policy (Tamil and English) and rejecting the three-language formula [5].

promise: Applying continuous pressure to abolish the NEET exam [5].

promise: Advocating for state languages to be the official language of respective High Courts [5].

section_name: Protection of Financial and State Rights of Tamil Nadu [4]
promise: Taking action to abolish the Governor's post and transferring Vice-Chancellor appointment powers to the state government [6].

Continuing archaeological excavations in 10 sites including Keeladi and Adichanallur [7].

Integrating the 'Thirukkural Development Scheme' into education with cash prizes for students [8].
Increasing the pension for Language Martyrs to ₹2,50,000 annually [9].
Developing Tamil Artificial Intelligence (AI) models and providing commercial rights to foreign countries free of charge [9].
Creating digital archives to protect Tamil arts, culture, and heritage [9].

Providing tax concessions and 50% renovation subsidies for Tamil Cultural Lodges (heritage buildings like Chettinad houses) [11].
Establishing a Coastal Corridor Tourism from Vedaranyam to Rameswaram [11].
Promoting Nilgiris Mountain Adventure Tourism, including a Mountain Biking Festival [11].
Developing Caravan Tourism with ₹2 Lakh subsidies for entrepreneurs [12].
Creating a Food Tourism Corridor to showcase traditional foods globally [12].
Introducing Riverfront Tourism along the Cauvery and Thamirabarani rivers [12].
Implementing a Tourism Skill Development Scheme [12].
Promoting Cinema Tourism via QR codes at popular filming locations [12].
Ensuring Tourism Safety with better infrastructure and 24/7 help centers [12].

Providing a 'Thaimaman Thanga Mothiram' maternity kit with a gold ring and essential items for every newborn in government hospitals [3].
Implementing the Kamarajar Education Guarantee Scheme, depositing ₹15,000 for students in grades 1-12 to prevent dropouts due to poverty [3].
Increasing the monthly financial assistance for widows and destitute women to ₹3,000 [3].
Launching the 'Singappenngal' development scheme, offering ₹5 Lakh collateral-free loans for women SHGs and entrepreneurs [3].
Ensuring 40% of government procurement for food, clothes, and school supplies is sourced from women's self-help groups and micro-enterprises [3].
Establishing specialized Special Industrial Estates tailored for women's needs in 10 major cities [3].

Integrating an AI-driven Centralized Monitoring Center with panic buttons in public transport and smart CCTV networks [4].
Establishing Special Fast-Track Courts to expedite justice for crimes against women [4].
Building modern hostels for working women and students across Tamil Nadu with a maximum monthly fee capped at ₹2,500 [4].
Providing a Youth Victory Fund offering ₹4,000 monthly for unemployed graduates and ₹2,500 for diploma/ITI holders for two years [5].
Ensuring total transparency in government recruitment and filling all vacancies within one year of notification [5].
Offering ₹20 Lakh collateral-free education loans for higher studies (UG to PhD) under the Education Guarantee Scheme [5].
Facilitating 'Victory Industrial Training' paid internships for 5 lakh youth annually, offering ₹10,000 stipends for graduates [5].
Providing ₹25 Lakh collateral-free loans under the Victory Entrepreneur Scheme [6].
Incentivizing private companies to hire 75% local youth by offering GST and electricity subsidies [6].
Establishing 'Micro Tech Parks' with high-speed internet and co-working spaces in 25 tier-2/tier-3 cities to support remote work [7].
Establishing a Tamil Nadu Sports University with global-standard sports excellence centers [7].
Launching the Tamil Nadu Olympic Scheme, offering up to ₹6 Crore for Olympic gold medalists and world-class training [7].
Building multi-sport stadiums and sports complexes in 10 zonal headquarters [8].
Identifying 500 young athletes at the school level for dedicated Olympic preparation [8].
Promoting coastal sports like Surfing and Kayaking, prioritizing coastal fishing communities [8].
Establishing a 100% Crop Insurance system utilizing agro-meteorological centers for precise loss calculation [9].
Supporting Copra coconut farmers with MSPs and value-added product corporations [9].
Positioning Tamil Nadu as an organic farming leader through a dedicated organic farming scheme and subsidies [9].
Providing free fertilizer for two years to small-scale farmers owning less than 2 acres [10].
Increasing the fishing ban period relief to ₹20,000 and monsoon relief to ₹7,000 [10].
Providing a ₹25 Lakh accident insurance policy for registered fishermen [10].
Increasing diesel subsidies for boats to 3,600 liters and establishing a Minimum Support Price (MSP) for fish [11].
Providing coastal housing schemes, ₹5 Lakh entrepreneurship loans, and inland fishing support [11].
Establishing seaweed parks, deep-sea fishing financial loans, and coastal erosion protection [12].
Allocating ₹5,000 Crore to expedite river interlinking projects across Tamil Nadu [13].
Implementing the 'Neeratchi Scheme' to restore 5 lakh new agricultural ponds [13].
Executing a ₹5,000 Crore Cauvery Delta Eco-restoration project to combat soil degradation and pollution [14].
Providing free goats and cows to 2 lakh families below the poverty line [15].
Raising the milk procurement price to ₹44 per liter and promoting A2 milk production [15].
Modernizing all public schools into smart classrooms with digital libraries and STEM labs by 2031 [16].
Aiming to make Tamil Nadu the #1 state in learning outcomes [16].
Establishing 100 'Kamarajar Special Schools' providing free holistic education for marginalized students [16].
Achieving a 0% dropout rate through AI tracking and integrated educational support [16].
Expanding the morning breakfast scheme up to the 12th standard [17].
Securing 10% horizontal reservation for government school students in all UG courses [17].
Setting up a Periyar Institute of Emerging Technologies (PIET) and introducing AI, VR, and AR modules into school curriculums [17, 18].
Targeting a $1.5 Trillion dollar economy by 2036 [19].
Drafting Industry Transformation Maps for the top 20 sectors and modernizing SIPCOT estates [19].
Implementing a ₹15,000 Crore debt relief fund to support struggling MSMEs [20].
Eliminating peak-hour electricity charges for MSMEs and establishing a ₹1,000 Crore Technology Upgradation Fund [20, 21].
Guaranteeing a 40% priority for MSMEs in government procurement [21].

section_name: Investment Attraction and District Balanced Growth
promise: Introducing a strict 21-day clearance guarantee for setting up mega industries and 7-15 days for MSMEs [21].

promise: Creating a specialized Investor Promotion Commission and a Non-Resident Tamil Investor Scheme [22].

section_name: Investment Attraction and District Balanced Growth
promise: Allocating a 'Backward District Development Budget' to eliminate regional disparities [23].

section_name: Investment Attraction and District Balanced Growth
promise: Establishing dedicated tech, textile, aerospace, EV, and food processing hubs categorized into Western, Kurinji, Northern, Central, and Southern zones [23-28].
Supporting 10,000 startups over 5 years with up to ₹50 Lakh funding under 'Start-Smart Tamil Nadu' [29].
Establishing a ₹500 Crore Venture Capital Fund for AI, Deep-Tech, and FinTech startups [29].
Developing EV & Battery Manufacturing zones, a commercial drone corridor, and an i-Tamil FinTech Corridor [30].
Creating an AVGC-XR Creative Economy ecosystem and an aerospace tech hub in Kulasekharapatnam [31].
Transforming Thoothukudi into a Green Hydrogen and green ammonia hub [32].
Providing free IVF (In Vitro Fertilization) treatments for childless couples in government hospitals.
Modernizing village hospitals with dialysis units, 4-bed ICUs, maternity labs, operation theaters, and rapid ambulances.
Offering high-quality medicines at 50% to 90% discounted rates through government pharmacies.
Launching the 'Happy Tamil Nadu Mission' to improve mental health, social harmony, and overall quality of life.
Implementing a Mental Health Medical Scheme with dedicated psychologists in communities and schools to address mental stress.
Establishing Tamil Nadu as a global identity for Siddha medicine, creating a Siddha University, and connecting hubs like Thiruvannamalai, Chidambaram, and Kutralam.
Allocating a ₹3,000 Crore Climate Budget to combat climate change and attract green investments.
Establishing an advanced early warning disaster management system, including 5 coastal radars and 38 district thermal cameras.
Creating a Sustainable Energy Economic Division under the Chief Minister's office to monitor carbon emissions and enforce green schemes.
Increasing Tamil Nadu's forest cover to 33% by 2031 through the 'Green Tamil Nadu Scheme', planting native trees like Sandalwood and Teak.
Implementing a strict zero-tolerance policy against discharging industrial waste into rivers, and establishing eco-parks along the Cauvery and Thamirabarani rivers.
Installing 50 km of solar fencing in conflict zones like Nilgiris and Coimbatore, and providing ₹10 Lakh compensation for wildlife-related deaths.
Protecting mangrove forests and removing encroachments from the Pallikaranai marshland.
Allocating a ₹500 Crore fund to combat severe air pollution in major cities.
Restoring the Cooum and Adyar rivers on a 'Mission-mode' basis.
Ensuring 24/7 uninterrupted electricity across Tamil Nadu by resolving overloaded feeders.
Providing substantial solar subsidies for households: ₹35,000 for 1kW, ₹70,000 for 2kW, and ₹1 Lakh for 3kW+.
Implementing a monthly electricity billing cycle, replacing the current bi-monthly system.
Reforming TANGEDCO to reduce AT&C losses from 16% to 10% and lowering electricity charges for apartments and MSMEs.
Operating 50% of public transport (5000 TNSTC buses) on renewable energy by 2031.
Expediting Chennai Metro Phase 2, starting Coimbatore Metro Phase 1, and preparing the DPR for Madurai Metro.
Expanding night services and cargo handling at airports through government-private partnerships.
Pushing for railway connectivity projects, including the 4th line from Tambaram to Chengalpattu, and connecting unserved regions.
Establishing multi-modal transit hubs integrating roads, rail, and air transport to reduce logistics costs by 20-30%.
Implementing AI-driven traffic management and offering 'Good Samaritan' awards for helping accident victims.
Allocating ₹20,000 Crore annually for pothole-free, well-maintained state and village highways.
Initiating ferry services to Sri Lanka and Malaysia to revive ancient Tamil sea trade routes.
Increasing the MGNREGA rural employment guarantee from 100 to 150 days.
Developing 5,000 'Model Villages' equipped with modern urban infrastructure, schools, and hospitals.
Creating 'Village Industry Clusters' to integrate local MSMEs and startups with supply chains.
Achieving 'Slum-Free Cities' by 2031, providing proper housing, drainage, and basic rights.
Protecting cultural cities (Thanjavur, Madurai, Kanchipuram, Mahabalipuram) with heritage conservation master plans.
Implementing a 'Climate-Resilient TN Master Plan 2045' to manage flood risks in vulnerable cities like Chennai, Thoothukudi, and Kanyakumari.
Reducing the severe property tax hikes previously imposed by the DMK government.
Introducing India's first 'No-Application' system where eligible citizens automatically receive welfare scheme benefits directly.
Implementing a Right to Service Act ensuring over 500 government services (certificates, patta, etc.) are delivered within a guaranteed timeframe.
Launching the 'Vettri Tamil Nadu Super App' to deliver birth certificates, ration cards, and voter IDs directly to mobile phones.
Establishing a 'Real-Time Governance Dashboard' for citizens to track government revenue, expenditures, and project statuses.
Creating 'Makkal Arangam', a digital platform where petitions gathering 10,000 signatures require direct government response, and 5 Lakh signatures prompt a legislative assembly debate.
Allocating 10% of district development funds to projects selected directly by citizens' votes.
Setting up a dedicated anti-corruption helpline and enacting a 'State Whistleblower Protection Act'.
Strengthening the Lokayukta to proactively investigate corruption charges against elected officials and public servants.
Abolishing sand smuggling through a transparent, online auction system.
Creating a 'Drug-Free Tamil Nadu' by introducing stringent laws to confiscate assets of drug peddlers and establishing a dedicated anti-drug force.
Closing TASMAC (liquor) shops in a phased manner and establishing rehabilitation centers to create a healthy, non-alcoholic state.
Ensuring independent investigations and severe legal action against officers involved in custodial deaths.
Publishing 5 crucial special financial reports covering Social Justice, Regional Development, Culture, Education, and Environment.
Releasing a 'White Paper' on State Finances to expose current debt levels and prevent further economic mismanagement.
Establishing the 'Tamil Nadu Strategic Investment Authority' (TNSIA) to bypass red tape and drive investments in AI, EV, Aerospace, and Semiconductors.
Restructuring Public Sector Undertakings (PSUs) to eliminate losses and introduce professional management practices.
